title: Glass and ceramics makers, decorators and finishers
description: Apply knowledge of engineering, biology, chemistry, computer science, and biomechanical principles to the design, development, and evaluation of biological, agricultural, and health systems and products, such as artificial organs, prostheses, instrumentation, medical information systems, and health management and care delivery systems. Promote worksite or product safety by applying knowledge of industrial processes, mechanics, chemistry, psychology, and industrial health and safety laws. Includes industrial product safety engineers. Research causes of fires, determine fire protection methods, and design or recommend materials or equipment such as structural components or fire-detection equipment to assist organizations in safeguarding life and property against fire, explosion, and related hazards. (Based on 46 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Confer with clients regarding price and type of arrangement desired and the date, time, and place of delivery; plan arrangement according to client's requirements, using knowledge of design and properties of materials, or select appropriate standard design pattern.

Repair broken parts, using precision hand tools and soldering irons; adjust lenses and frames to correct alignment; trim, scrape, or deburr objects or parts, using chisels, scrapers, and other hand tools and equipment; clean and maintain tools and equipment, using solvents, brushes, and rags; and repair and maintain equipment, objects, or parts, using hand tools.

Inspect lens blanks to detect flaws, verify smoothness of surface, and ensure thickness of coating on lenses; examine prescriptions, work orders, or broken or used eyeglasses to determine specifications for lenses, contact lenses, or other optical elements; and inspect, weigh, and measure mounted or unmounted lenses after completion to verify alignment and conformance to specifications, using precision instruments.

Clean finished lenses and eyeglasses, using cloths and solvents; control equipment that coats lenses to alter their reflective qualities; water plants, and cut, condition, and clean flowers and foliage for storage; and maintain portfolios of artistic work to demonstrate styles, interests, and abilities.

Select lens blanks, molds, tools, and polishing or grinding wheels according to production specifications; set up machines to polish, bevel, edge, or grind lenses, flats, blanks, or other precision optical elements; position and adjust cutting tools to specified curvature, dimensions, and depth of cut; and feed stock into cutting machines, onto conveyors, or under cutting blades, by threading, guiding, pushing, or turning handwheels.

Set dials and start machines to polish lenses or hold lenses against rotating wheels to polish them manually; move controls to adjust, start, or stop equipment during grinding and polishing processes; and start machines to verify setups, and make any necessary adjustments.

Assemble eyeglass frames and attach shields, nose pads, and temple pieces, using pliers, screwdrivers, and drills; mount, secure, and align finished lenses in frames or optical assemblies, using precision hand tools; and integrate and develop visual elements, such as line, space, mass, color, and perspective, to produce desired effects, such as the illustration of ideas, emotions, or moods.

Deliver arrangements to customers, or oversee employees responsible for deliveries; perform office and retail service duties, such as keeping financial records, serving customers, answering telephones, selling giftware items, and receiving payment; and wrap and price completed arrangements.

Remove lenses from molds and separate lenses in containers for further processing or storage; unpack stock as it comes into the shop; and direct work crews in the cleaning and repair of furnace walls and flooring.

Decorate or supervise the decoration of buildings, halls, churches, or other facilities for parties, weddings, and other occasions; create and change in-store and window displays, designs, and looks to enhance a shop's image; and conduct classes or demonstrations, or train other workers.

Weigh or measure materials, ingredients, or products to ensure conformance to requirements; inspect, examine, or test materials or products visually or with hands, or collect samples for laboratory testing to ensure conformance to established standards.
Operate or tend machines to mix or blend a wide variety of materials, and control equipment such as ladles, grinding wheels, or conveyors to move materials through production processes.
Clean and maintain equipment, and monitor operations of equipment to ensure efficiency and safety, including troubleshooting and adjusting machinery according to specifications.
Record operational or production data, including specifications, and maintain production logs to ensure that production processes meet standards and specifications.
Cut, shape, and trim materials such as glass and ceramics using hand tools and power tools, and mark cutting lines according to specifications.
Handle raw materials by transporting them between work areas using carts, hoists, or hand tools, and loading machinery for processing.
Adjust machine controls to regulate functions such as temperature and pressure, and operate machinery used in production processes to fabricate products according to specifications.
Mix or prepare materials for processing by weighing, measuring, or adding chemicals, and monitoring and conducting test runs to ensure product quality.
Dislodge and clear jammed materials from machinery, and resolve production problems by conferring with supervisors or other equipment operators.
Prepare and package finished products or mixtures for storage and shipment, ensuring they meet quality standards and specifications.
Review and follow work orders and specifications to determine necessary materials, procedures, and adjustments for production processes.
Mark identification numbers or symbols on products, and inspect and discard defective items to ensure quality control in production.
Gather and send product samples to laboratories for analysis to verify compliance with specifications and standards.
Use various hand tools and machinery to process glass and ceramic materials, including shaping and polishing techniques to finish products.
Study technical documents, blueprints, or CAD designs to plan operations and design tools, jigs, or fixtures for production.
Lay out and mark reference points and dimensions on materials, using measuring instruments and drawing or scribing tools.
Cut, shape, and form metal parts or other materials using lathes, power saws, snips, brakes, shears, files, and mallets.
Inspect and test products to verify conformance to specifications, using precision measuring instruments or circuit testers.
Set up and operate machines, such as lathes, drill presses, punch presses, or bandsaws, to fabricate prototypes or models.
Study blueprints, drawings, and sketches to determine material dimensions, required equipment, and operations sequences.
Calibrate devices and instruments for accurate measurement, including liquid flow devices and meters.
Align, fit, and join parts using bolts and screws or by welding or gluing, and construct platforms, fixtures, and jigs for holding patterns.
Monitor and record the performance of equipment and processing data for quality control and assurance in biofuels processing.
Collect and analyze samples, and perform routine tests or laboratory analyses to assess quality for biofuels and other materials.
Operate processing equipment for biofuels production, including valves, pumps, engines, or generators for controlling production processes.
Perform routine maintenance on mechanical, electrical, or electronic equipment used in processing.
Assemble pattern sections, using hand tools, bolts, screws, rivets, glue, or welding equipment, and construct tools, dies, molds, jigs, and fixtures.
Create mechanical design documents for parts, assemblies, and finished products, and develop engineering specifications for processes.
Document engineering processes, including creating or maintaining formal engineering documents, training manuals, and project records.
Review specifications and recommend engineering or manufacturing changes to achieve design objectives or improve processes.
Conduct training or provide consultation on engineering processes or equipment usage to ensure proper operation.
Analyze existing development or manufacturing procedures and suggest improvements to optimize efficiency and reduce waste.
Promote awareness or use of alternative or renewable energy sources and assist in environmental compliance activities relating to energy use.
Conduct environmental studies or audits on topics related to manufacturing processes or energy efficiency.
Remove completed workpieces and manage product documentation throughout the manufacturing or production process.
Design and develop processes for waste reduction and energy conservation within manufacturing systems.
Test or evaluate prototype models to optimize designs for function, efficiency, or safety standards.
Develop or implement controls for mechanical systems or introduce automation into industrial tasks to enhance productivity.
Conduct tests or validations to ensure equipment and systems conform to safety or operational standards.
Create checklists or standards for quality assurance in manufacturing, including solar installation and photonics production.
Analyze, interpret, or create graphical representations of engineering data and report findings for review.
Research new technologies in glass and ceramics, ensuring modern applications enhance quality and performance.

Determine commercial, industrial, scientific, or other uses for electro-optical applications or devices; develop laser-processed designs such as laser-cut medical devices and design laser machining equipment for high-speed ablation. Additionally, create back-ups of robot programs or parameters, debug robotics programs, and design or engineer robotic systems, such as automatic vehicle control and advanced displays.

Design electro-optical sensing or imaging systems; analyze, fabricate, or test fiber-optic links; and investigate mechanical failures or unexpected maintenance problems. Provide technical support for robotic systems and supervise technologists, technicians, or other engineers.

Evaluate robotic systems or prototypes; inspect etched work for depth of etching, uniformity, and defects; and prepare technical reports, data summary documents, or research articles for scientific publication, regulatory submissions, or patent applications. Additionally, document robotic application development and maintenance, and record times for parts removed from furnaces to ensure proper heat treatment.

Conduct research on fire retardants, develop plans for the prevention of destruction by fire, wind, and water, evaluate fire department performance regarding fire safety, and develop training materials and conduct safety training. Advise architects and builders on fire prevention equipment and techniques.

Use computer software to design patterns for engraving; conduct or oversee the conduct of prototype development; and establish analytical procedures for characterizing and testing nanomaterials. Additionally, inspect facilities, machinery, or safety equipment for potential hazards and ensure compliance with safety regulations.

Conduct research related to a range of nanotechnology topics; create designs or prototypes for nanosystem applications; and develop processes or identify equipment needed for pilot or commercial nanoscale production. Furthermore, provide scientific or technical guidance on micro and nanoscale systems to relevant personnel.

Inspect buildings or designs to determine fire protection system requirements, develop methodologies for transferring procedures or biological processes from labs to commercial-scale manufacturing production, and maintain liaisons with outside organizations to facilitate emergency responses. Recommend changes to improve safety standards and evaluate health hazards that could occur from product misuse.

Conduct analyses addressing issues such as failure, reliability, or yield improvement; recommend procedures for detection, prevention, and elimination of physical, chemical, or other product hazards; and participate in product usage and precautionary label instruction preparation.

Review plans and specifications for construction of new machinery or equipment to ensure compliance with safety requirements and consult with chemists or biologists to develop or evaluate novel technologies. Maintain databases of experiment characteristics or results, and evaluate adequacy of employee safety programs.

Review existing manufacturing processes to identify opportunities for yield improvement or reduced process variation; lead studies to examine or recommend changes; and communicate with suppliers regarding the design specifications of bioproduction equipment, instrumentation, or materials.

Conduct research to evaluate safety levels for products; develop industry standards for product safety; and investigate industrial accidents, injuries, or occupational diseases to determine causes and preventive measures.

Design fire detection equipment, alarm systems, and fire extinguishing devices; advise organizations on how to handle health-related problems; and provide expert testimony in litigation cases related to product safety.
Prepare reports documenting fire prevention and protection issues; write proposals to secure external funding; and manage teams of engineers to create schedules and track inventories.
Clean oxides and scales from parts or fittings, using steam sprays or chemical and water baths.
Adjust controls to maintain temperatures and heating times, using thermal instruments and charts, dials and gauges of furnaces, and color of stock in furnaces to make setting determinations.
Set and adjust speeds of reels and conveyors for prescribed time cycles to pass parts through continuous furnaces.
Operate gas or electric kilns to fire pottery pieces.
Mix and apply glazes to pottery pieces, using tools, such as spray guns.
Attach handles to pottery pieces.
Pack and ship pottery to stores or galleries for retail sale.
Design spaces to display pottery for sale.
Pour, pack, spread, or press plaster, concrete, or other materials into or around models or molds.
Prepare work for sale or exhibition, and maintain relationships with retail, pottery, art, and resource networks that can facilitate sale or exhibition of work.
Clean and polish engraved areas.
Identify and recommend energy savings strategies to achieve more energy-efficient operation.
Conduct tests of nuclear fuel behavior and cycles or performance of nuclear machinery and equipment to optimize performance of existing plants.
Check or monitor energy-related design or construction issues, such as energy engineering, energy management, or sustainable design.

Engrave and print patterns, designs, etchings, trademarks, or lettering onto flat or curved surfaces of a wide variety of metal, glass, plastic, or paper items, using hand tools or hand-held power tools.
Verify depths and dimensions of cuts or carvings to ensure adherence to specifications, blueprints, or models, using measuring instruments.

Operate drying chambers to dry or finish molded ceramic ware, and move pieces from wheels to allow them to dry. Examine finished ware for defects and measure dimensions using a rule and thickness gauge. Perform test-fires of pottery to achieve specific colors and textures. Maintain supplies of tools, equipment, and materials, ordering additional supplies as needed.

Adjust pressures, temperatures, and trimming tool settings as required. Start machine units and conveyors, observing lights and gauges on panel boards to verify operational efficiency. Adjust wheel speeds according to the feel of the clay to shape and form wares using hands and tools. Verify accuracy of shapes and sizes of objects using calipers and templates. Pull wires through bases of articles and wheels to separate finished pieces. Smooth surfaces of finished pieces using rubber scrapers and wet sponges. Cut, shape, fit, join, mold, or otherwise process materials using hand or power tools.

Design clay forms, molds, and decorations, and create functional or decorative objects by hand using various methods and materials. Develop concepts or creative ideas for craft objects, and create prototypes or models. Confer with customers to assess their needs or obtain feedback. Select materials based on specific characteristics such as strength, color, texture, and malleability.

Develop designs using specialized computer software. Research craft trends, venues, and customer buying patterns to inspire designs and marketing strategies. Advertise products and craft works using various media. Plan and attend craft shows to market products.

Inspect materials, products, or equipment to detect defects or malfunctions, and clean work areas. Enforce safety and sanitation regulations and ensure workers conform to production or processing standards. Conduct employee training in relevant operational or safety procedures. Maintain operations data and prepare management reports of production results.

Align and secure pattern film on reference tables and observe printed circuit boards. Write programs in the language of machines' controllers, modifying as needed to enhance efficiency and eliminate errors. Determine setup procedures and select machine dies accordingly.

Operate finishing machines to grind, drill, sand, bevel, decorate, wash, or polish glass products. Shape, bend, or join sections of glass using specialized tools. Develop sketches into blueprint specifications applying knowledge of glass technology. Repair and maintain equipment used in glass fabrication. Operate machines to control processes of glass formation and ensure products conform to specifications.
Acquire and maintain inventory of materials and tools required for production. Order tooling and supplies based on job requirements.

Define material specifications for products, determining types and quantities of materials needed. Inspect and measure raw materials and finished products to verify compliance with specifications, making adjustments as necessary. Monitor and maintain machine cycles to prevent deficiencies and ensure quality control in production processes.

Design glass objects using blowpipes and hand tools, placing glass into molds and controlling presses for product formation. Remove scratches and polish surfaces of glass products. Create functional and decorative glassware components. Develop approaches for glass production processes, ensuring efficiency and quality.

emerging_tasks.task: Tint lenses according to customer specifications.
Read blueprints and project specification manuals to recommend appropriate hardware and electronic locking devices.
Develop or modify building codes and fire codes.
Estimate energy production by analyzing wind data.
Perform testing on fire protection equipment or building features, such as fire doors or smoke control systems.

Mix chemicals according to recipes to create glazes.
